---
title: "Check Git status before commit"
subtitle: "BNR info-hub Technical Manual"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Check Git status before commit

## Purpose

This guide explains how to check Git status before committing changes to the BNR `info-hub` repository.

The goal is to commit only intended, safe, useful files.

## Basic command

From the repo root:

```powershell
cd C:\yoshimi-hot\output\analyse-bnr\info-hub
git status
```

# Stage 1: Review changed files

:::{.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Step 1. Read the status output

Git will show files that are:

- modified
- deleted
- untracked
- staged
:::

:::{.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Files that are usually expected

These may be expected after a briefing workflow update:

```text
scripts/stata/...
outputs/public/...
site/downloads/files/...
site/surveillance/...
site/technical/...
CHANGELOG.md
DECISIONS.md
```
:::

# Stage 2: Watch for files that should not be committed

:::{.callout-warning collapse="true"}
## Do not commit these

```text
site/_site/
site/.quarto/
local setup notes
private data
private logs
private prepared .dta files
scripts/stata/config/bnr_paths_LOCAL.do
```
:::

:::{.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Why `_site` should not be committed

`site/_site/` is generated by Quarto.

It is a build output, not source material.
:::

# Stage 3: Check ignored files if needed

:::{.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Step 1. Check whether a file is ignored

Example:

```powershell
git check-ignore -v scripts\stata\config\bnr_paths_LOCAL.do
```
:::

# Stage 4: Stage only intended files

:::{.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Step 1. Use selective staging

Avoid blindly staging everything if you are unsure.

```powershell
git add path\to\file
```
:::

:::{.callout-warning collapse="true"}
## Avoid careless bulk staging

Do not use:

```powershell
git add .
```

unless you have carefully reviewed all changed and untracked files.
:::
